title: malva flava
intro: Malva flava, also known as marshmallow, is a perennial herb belonging to the mallow family that is native to Europe, Asia, and Africa. The plant is rich in mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that have soothing and protective properties, making it a popular medicinal herb. Malva flava has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Malva flava can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or topical cream, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote healthy digestion, soothe sore throats, and calm irritated skin.
